title: Strategy with mutable default and concrete-type leak
instructions: Review this diff and report all issues as JSON findings with severity/file/line/rationale.
diff:
diff --git a/retry.py b/retry.py
--- a/retry.py
+++ b/retry.py
@@ -1,3 +1,34 @@
+class FixedBackoff:
+    def __init__(self, delay, max_attempts):
+        self.delay = delay
+        self.max_attempts = max_attempts
+    def next_delay(self, attempt):
+        return self.delay if attempt < self.max_attempts else None
+
+
+def run_with_retry(func, strategy, history=[]):
+    attempt = 0
+    while True:
+        try:
+            return func()
+        except Exception:
+            if not isinstance(strategy, FixedBackoff):
+                raise
+            delay = strategy.next_delay(attempt)
+            if delay is None:
+                raise
+            history.append(delay)
+            attempt += 1

title: Context manager suppresses exceptions and leaks on error
instructions: Review this diff and report all issues as JSON findings with severity/file/line/rationale.
diff:
diff --git a/lease.py b/lease.py
--- a/lease.py
+++ b/lease.py
@@ -1,2 +1,26 @@
+class LeaseGuard:
+    def __init__(self, pool):
+        self.pool = pool
+        self.lease_id = None
+
+    def __enter__(self):
+        self.lease_id = self.pool.acquire()
+        return self.lease_id
+
+    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
+        if exc_type is None:
+            self.pool.release(self.lease_id)
+        return True
+
+
+def do_work(pool, body):
+    with LeaseGuard(pool):
+        body()

title: God object: store mixes persistence, HTTP, and formatting
instructions: Review this diff and report all issues as JSON findings with severity/file/line/rationale.
diff:
diff --git a/marks_store.py b/marks_store.py --- a/marks_store.py +++ b/marks_store.py @@ -1,4 +1,41 @@ import sqlite3 +import urllib.request + + +class MarkManager: + """Owns the marks DB, talks to the sync API, and renders HTML.""" + def __init__(self, db_path, api_url): + self.con = sqlite3.connect(db_path) + self.api_url = api_url + self.con.execute("CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS marks(task_id TEXT, rating INT)") + + def add(self, task_id, rating): + self.con.execute("INSERT INTO marks VALUES (?, ?)", (task_id, rating)) + self.con.commit() + + def sync_to_server(self): + rows = self.con.execute("SELECT task_id, rating FROM marks").fetchall() + for tid, rating in rows: + urllib.request.urlopen(self.api_url + "?id=" + tid) + + def render_html(self): + rows = self.con.execute("SELECT task_id, rating FROM marks").fetchall() + html = "" + for tid, rating in rows: + html += "" + return html + "
" + tid + "" + str(rating) + "
"

title: Builder mutates self and shares list across forks
instructions: Review this diff and report all issues as JSON findings with severity/file/line/rationale.
diff:
diff --git a/query.py b/query.py
--- a/query.py
+++ b/query.py
@@ -1,2 +1,30 @@
+class QueryBuilder:
+    def __init__(self, conds=None):
+        self.table = None
+        self.conds = conds if conds is not None else []
+
+    def from_(self, table):
+        self.table = table
+        return self
+
+    def where(self, cond):
+        self.conds.append(cond)
+        return self
+
+    def fork(self):
+        # Reuse current conditions as a base for a divergent query.
+        return QueryBuilder(self.conds)
+
+    def build(self):
+        sql = "SELECT * FROM " + self.table
+        if self.conds:
+            sql += " WHERE " + " AND ".join(self.conds)
+        return sql
